source_refs.source_quote: Your heating contractor will have set the system to the required operating pressure of at least 15 psi (1 bar) and entered the setting in Tab. 1, page 5.
Your heating contractor will have set the system to the required operating pressure of at least 15 psi (1 bar) and entered the setting in Tab. 1, page 5.
Maximum heating system operating pressure (standard = 30 psi)

source_refs.section_title: Topping up the boiler water and bleeding the system
source_refs.source_quote: Only fill the heating system when cold (the flow temperature should be no more than 100 °F).

fault_codes.description: Burner fault indicated by the fault indicator lamp on the burner.
cautions_or_notes: Always follow the burner manufacturer's instructions.
Too many resets can damage the burner's ignition transformer.
Do not press the reset button more than three times in a row.
If the fault does not reset after the third attempt, localize and rectify the fault with the help of the burner documentation.
Notify a service contractor if necessary.
symptoms: Fault indicator lamp on the burner comes on

source_refs.section_title: Identifying operating modes and resetting faults
source_refs.source_quote: If there is a burner fault the fault indicator lamp on the burner comes on (→ burner documentation). Such faults can generally be reset by pressing the reset button on the burner.
Press reset button on burner.

CAUTION: Risk of system damage due to too many resets. Always follow the burner manufacturer's instructions. Too many resets can damage the bur-ner's ignition transformer. Do not press the reset button more than three times in a row. If the fault does not reset after the third attempt, localize and rectify the fault with the help of the burner documentation. Notify a service contractor if necessary.

fault_codes.description: Faults indicated on the control unit display.
cautions_or_notes: More detailed information can be found in the controls' documentation.
symptoms: Indicated on the control unit display

source_refs.section_title: Identifying operating modes and resetting faults
source_refs.source_quote: Controls and heating system faults are indicated on the control unit display if it has one. More detailed information can be found in the controls' documentation.

fault_codes.description: The blocked vent switch (WMO) has interrupted the burner due to an obstructed flue gas pipe.
manufacturer_steps: Call your boiler service technician if you suspect the WMO may have tripped.
cautions_or_notes: Canadian installations of this boiler are required to be equipped with a blocked vent switch (WMO).

source_refs.source_quote: Canadian installations of this boiler are required to be equipped with a blocked vent switch (WMO) that interrupts the burner in case of an obstructed flue gas pipe. Call your boiler service technician if you suspect the WMO may have tripped.

safety_warnings.text: If these instructions are not followed exactly, a fire or explosion may be caused with serious property damage or loss of life and serious injury. Do not store or use gasoline or any other flammable liquids or vapors in the vicinity of this system or any other heating system.

source_refs.source_quote: DANGER! If these instructions are not followed exactly, a fire or explosion may be caused with serious property damage or loss of life and serious injury. Do not store or use gasoline or any other flammable liquids or vapors in the vicinity of this system or any other heating system.

safety_warnings.text: Caution indicates that minor damage to property may occur.

source_refs.source_quote: Caution indicates that minor damage to property may occur.

safety_warnings.text: Warning indicates that minor personal injury or severe damage to property may occur.

source_refs.source_quote: Warning indicates that minor personal injury or severe damage to property may occur.

safety_warnings.text: Danger means that severe personal injury may occur. Very serious cases may result in death.

source_refs.source_quote: Danger means that severe personal injury may occur. Very serious cases may result in death.

safety_warnings.text: If you smell flue gas: Switch off the boiler (→ page 6). Open windows and doors. Inform an authorized heating contractor.

source_refs.source_quote:
If you smell flue gas
► Switch off the boiler (→ page 6).
► Open windows and doors.
► Inform an authorized heating contractor.

safety_warnings.text: Correct and proper installation and adjustment of the burner and the controls are the fundamental requirements for safe and economical operation of the boiler. The boiler may only be installed or converted by an approved heating contractor. Do not modify any parts that carry flue gas. The hot water tank may only be used for heating domestic hot water. Never shut off safety valves! Water must be able to escape from the safety valve for the hot water system and piping when the water is being heated.

source_refs.source_quote:
Installation
► Correct and proper installation and adjustment of the burner and the controls are the fundamental requirements for safe and economical operation of the boiler.
► The boiler may only be installed or converted by an approved heating contractor.
► Do not modify any parts that carry flue gas.
► The hot water tank may only be used for heating domestic hot water.
► Never shut off safety valves! Water must be able to escape from the safety valve for the hot water system and piping when the water is being heated.

safety_warnings.text: Servicing and repairs may only be carried out by a trained heating contractor. Have any faults immediately corrected in order to prevent damage to the system. The operator is responsible for the general and environmental safety of the heating system. Use only genuine Buderus spare parts. Damage caused by the use of parts not supplied by Buderus is not covered by the Buderus warranty.

source_refs.source_quote:
Recommendation for users:
► Servicing and repairs may only be carried out by a trained heating contractor.
► Have any faults immediately corrected in order to prevent damage to the system.
► The operator is responsible for the general and environmental safety of the heating system.
► Use only genuine Buderus spare parts. Damage caused by the use of parts not supplied by Buderus is not covered by the Buderus warranty.

safety_warnings.text: Never put yourself at risk. Your own safety must always take the highest priority.

source_refs.source_quote:
Danger by failing to consider your own safety in an emergency such as a fire
► Never put yourself at risk. Your own safety must always take the highest priority.

safety_warnings.text: Any work on components that carry oil may only be carried out by a certified heating contractor. Do not use or store flammable (paper, thinners, paints, etc.) near the boiler. Maintain a clearance of at least 16 inches from the boiler.

source_refs.source_quote:
Dangers posed by explosive and flammable materials
► Any work on components that carry oil may only be carried out by a certified heating contractor.
► Do not use or store flammable (paper, thinners, paints, etc.) near the boiler.
► Maintain a clearance of at least 16 inches from the boiler.

safety_warnings.text: Insufficient ventilation may cause dangerous flue gas leaks. Never close off or reduce the size of air inlet or outlet vents. The boiler must not be operated until the obstruction has been removed.

source_refs.source_quote:
Risk of poisoning. Insufficient ventilation may cause dangerous flue gas leaks.
► Never close off or reduce the size of air inlet or outlet vents.
► The boiler must not be operated until the obstruction has been removed.

safety_warnings.text: Carbon monoxide (CO) is a poisonous gas, which arises during the incomplete combustion of fossil fuels such as oil, gas or solid fuels. Dangers arise, if carbon monoxide escapes from the heating system due to a fault or a leak and collects unnoticed in enclosed spaces. You can neither see, taste nor smell carbon monoxide. To avoid danger from carbon monoxide: Have the heating system inspected and serviced regularly by an approved contractor. Use a CO detector, which gives an alarm in good time if CO escapes. If you suspect a CO leak: Warn your neighbours and leave the building immediately. Call an approved contractor. Have any defects rectified.

source_refs.source_quote:
Danger to life from carbon monoxide
Carbon monoxide (CO) is a poisonous gas, which arises during the incomplete combustion of fossil fuels such as oil, gas or solid fuels.
Dangers arise, if carbon monoxide escapes from the heating system due to a fault or a leak and collects unnoticed in enclosed spaces.
You can neither see, taste nor smell carbon monoxide.
To avoid danger from carbon monoxide:
► Have the heating system inspected and serviced regularly by an approved contractor.
► Use a CO detector, which gives an alarm in good time if CO escapes.
► If you suspect a CO leak:
Warn your neighbours and leave the building immediately.
Call an approved contractor.
Have any defects rectified.

safety_warnings.text: Do not use the appliance if any part of it has been under water. Call in a qualified customer service technician immediately to check the appliance and replace any controls that have been under water.

source_refs.source_quote:
Risk of water damage
► Do not use the appliance if any part of it has been under water.
► Call in a qualified customer service technician immediately to check the appliance and replace any controls that have been under water.

safety_warnings.text: Keep the combustion air free of corrosive substances (e. g. halogenated hydrocarbons that contain chlorine or fluorine compounds). In that way you will prevent corrosion.

source_refs.source_quote:
Combustion air
► Keep the combustion air free of corrosive substances (e. g. halogenated hydrocarbons that contain chlorine or fluorine compounds). In that way you will prevent corrosion.

safety_warnings.text: The operator must read the information on how the boiler works and have the heating system installer explain how to operate it.

source_refs.source_quote:
Instructing the operator
► The operator must read the information on how the boiler works and have the heating system installer explain how to operate it.

safety_warnings.text: Health risk from contaminated domestic water. It is imperative that you observe all regulations and standards applicable in your country regarding prevention of domestic water contamination.

source_refs.section_title: Topping up the boiler water and bleeding the system
source_refs.source_quote: CAUTION: Health risk from contaminated domestic water. ► It is imperative that you observe all regulations and standards applicable in your country regarding prevention of domestic water contamination.

safety_warnings.topic: System damage due to temperature stress
safety_warnings.text: If you fill the heating system when it is hot, the resulting temperature stress can cause stress cracks and a leak in the boiler. Only fill the heating system when cold (the flow temperature should be no more than 100 °F).

source_refs.section_title: Topping up the boiler water and bleeding the system
source_refs.source_quote: CAUTION: Risk of damage to system due to temperature stress. If you fill the heating system when it is hot, the resulting temperature stress can cause stress cracks and a leak in the boiler. Only fill the heating system when cold (the flow temperature should be no more than 100 °F).

safety_warnings.topic: System damage due to frequent topping up
safety_warnings.text: If you have to top up the heating water frequently, the heating system may suffer damage from corrosion or scaling due to repeated introduction of oxygen. Ask your heating contractor if the local water can be used untreated or whether it needs to be treated. Notify your heating contractor if you find you need to top up your heating system frequently, as there may be a leak.

source_refs.section_title: Topping up the boiler water and bleeding the system
source_refs.source_quote: CAUTION: Risk of system damage due to frequent topping up. If you have to top up the heating water frequently, the heating system may suffer damage from corrosion or scaling due to repeated introduction of oxygen. ► Ask your heating contractor if the local water can be used untreated or whether it needs to be treated. ► Notify your heating contractor if you find you need to top up your heating system frequently, as there may be a leak.

safety_warnings.text: If the heating system has been switched off, it may freeze up. Leave the heating system permanently switched on as much as possible. Protect your heating system against freezing by draining the heating system and hot water pipes at the lowest point.

source_refs.source_quote: CAUTION: Risk of system damage from freezing. If the heating system has been switched off, it may freeze up. ► Leave the heating system permanently switched on as much as possible. Protect your heating system against freezing by draining the heating system and hot water pipes at the lowest point.

safety_warnings.text: Only use the fuel specified. The correct fuel is entered in Tab. 2 by the heating system installer.

source_refs.source_quote: CAUTION: Risk of system damage from use of incorrect fuel. Only use the fuel specified. The correct fuel is entered in Tab. 2 by the heating system installer.

safety_warnings.topic: Boiler damage from contaminated combustion air
safety_warnings.text: Keep the combustion air free of corrosive substances (e.g. halogenated hydrocarbons that contain chlorine or fluorine compounds). Prevent heavy accumulation of dust.

source_refs.source_quote: CAUTION: Risk of boiler damage from contaminated combustion air. ► Keep the combustion air free of corrosive substances (e.g. halogenated hydrocarbons that contain chlorine or fluorine compounds). Prevent heavy accumulation of dust.

safety_warnings.text: In the event of severe risk of flooding, disconnect the boiler from its power supply and shut off the fuel supply before water enters the boiler room. After the flood has subsided, ask your installer to check the heating system before starting it up again. All parts that have been in contact with flood water must be replaced by a qualified heating contractor.

source_refs.source_quote: CAUTION: Risk of system damage from water. ► In the event of severe risk of flooding, disconnect the boiler from its power supply and shut off the fuel supply before water enters the boiler room. ► After the flood has subsided, ask your installer to check the heating system before starting it up again. ► All parts that have been in contact with flood water must be replaced by a qualified heating contractor.

safety_warnings.topic: System damage due to lack of or inadequate cleaning and maintenance
safety_warnings.text: Have your heating system inspected, cleaned and maintained by a heating contractor once a year. We recommend you sign a contract covering an annual inspection and maintenance on an as-required basis.

source_refs.source_quote: CAUTION: Risk of system damage due to lack of or inadequate cleaning and maintenance. ► Have your heating system inspected, cleaned and maintained by a heating contractor once a year. ► We recommend you sign a contract covering an annual inspection and maintenance on an as-required basis.

safety_warnings.topic: System damage due to too many resets
safety_warnings.text: Always follow the burner manufacturer's instructions. Too many resets can damage the burner's ignition transformer. Do not press the reset button more than three times in a row. If the fault does not reset after the third attempt, localize and rectify the fault with the help of the burner documentation. Notify a service contractor if necessary.

source_refs.source_quote: CAUTION: Risk of system damage due to too many resets. Always follow the burner manufacturer's instructions. Too many resets can damage the bur-ner's ignition transformer. Do not press the reset button more than three times in a row. If the fault does not reset after the third attempt, localize and rectify the fault with the help of the burner documentation. Notify a service contractor if necessary.

safety_warnings.text: The heating system can freeze up in cold weather if it has been switched off or disabled by a fault shutdown. Rectify the fault immediately and restart the heating system. If that is not possible, protect the heating system against freezing by draining it at the lowest point.

source_refs.section_title: Identifying operating modes and resetting faults
source_refs.source_quote: CAUTION: Risk of system damage from freezing. The heating system can freeze up in cold weather if it has been switched off or disabled by a fault shutdown. ► Rectify the fault immediately and restart the heating system. ► If that is not possible, protect the heating system against freezing by draining it at the lowest point.

maintenance_tasks.description: To maintain a high level of efficiency, operate economically, and maintain cleanest combustion.

source_refs.source_quote:
Heating systems should be regularly maintained for the following reasons:
• to maintain a high level of efficiency and to operate the system economically (low fuel consumption),
• to achieve a high level of operational reliability,
• to maintain the cleanest combustion.
► sign a maintenance and servicing contract with an approved heating contractor covering annual servicing and maintenance.
► The flue system must be inspected once a year. Have all parts that show any signs of damage from corrosion or other causes replaced.
► The boiler must be serviced by qualified boiler service company once a year. Servicing must include the burner, the entire flue and combustion air system and any air inlet/outlet vents. All parts that show any signs of damage from corrosion or other causes must be replaced.

maintenance_tasks.description: Inspect the flue system and replace any damaged parts.

source_refs.source_quote: ► The flue system must be inspected once a year. Have all parts that show any signs of damage from corrosion or other causes replaced.

maintenance_tasks.description: Servicing must include the burner, the entire flue and combustion air system and any air inlet/outlet vents. All parts that show any signs of damage from corrosion or other causes must be replaced.

source_refs.source_quote: ► The boiler must be serviced by qualified boiler service company once a year. Servicing must include the burner, the entire flue and combustion air system and any air inlet/outlet vents. All parts that show any signs of damage from corrosion or other causes must be replaced.

maintenance_tasks.description: Sign a contract covering annual inspection and maintenance.

source_refs.source_quote: ► sign a maintenance and servicing contract with an approved heating contractor covering annual servicing and maintenance.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Heating system inspection, cleaning and maintenance

source_refs.source_quote: ► Have your heating system inspected, cleaned and maintained by a heating contractor once a year.

maintenance_tasks.task_name: Sign annual inspection and maintenance contract

source_refs.source_quote: ► We recommend you sign a contract covering an annual inspection and maintenance on an as-required basis.
